sourceMovie.title: Dumb Money
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dumb Money (2023) stuck because it commits to its history premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Craig Gillespie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The history and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dumb Money clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dumb Money?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, The Big Short, Thank You for Smoking—each shares DNA with Dumb Money while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dumb Money?
faq.answer: Dumb Money (2023) is best described as History, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dumb Money worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
